name: Azorean Portuguese
notes: Azorean Portuguese (reference: Terceira Island; São Miguel sub-variety for u→y feature). Categorical features per DIALECT_PATTERNS / whitepaper5 (TigreGotico internal dialect research): (1) STRESSED u → [y] — most salient feature (São Miguel class): tu→[ty], número→[ˈnymɨɾu]; encoded in positional_graphemes u/nucleus_stressed→[y]; this is São Miguel-class (strongest realisation); Terceira shows partial realisation; (2) /ow/ PRESERVED — ou diphthong retained where Lisbon monophthongizes (ouro→[ˈowɾu]); (3) NASAL DIPHTHONG → NASAL+N — plural -ões→[õns], -ães→[ɐ̃ns] (shared with Madeira); (4) CONSERVATIVE VOWEL PRESERVATION — unstressed /e/ may surface as [e] rather than [ɨ]; encoded in allophone ə→[ɨ, e]; (5) RAISED NASAL DIPHTHONG — ão→[õw̃] (already present). NOTES-ONLY: boi→[bô], pastar→[pɐʃˈtâ] — lexical; e→i extreme reduction — optional island-specific; sporadic l-palatalization; inter-island variation is substantial. Sources: DIALECT_PATTERNS.md §Açores Fronted u, §Nasal Patterns, §Special Cases; whitepaper5.

ancestors.notes: Standard European Portuguese; Azorean diverges in stressed u→y (São Miguel), nasal diphthong reduction (→nasal+n), and /ow/ preservation

sources.author: Boléo, M.P.
sources.title: Estudos de linguística portuguesa e românica

sources.author: TigreGotico
sources.title: DIALECT_PATTERNS.md and whitepaper5: Phoneme-Level Dialect Transforms for European Portuguese
sources.notes: Internal dialect feature matrix and IPA transform inventory; pending external peer review

stress.notes: Acordo Ortográfico 1990 conventions: paroxytone default; written accents override; final -i/-u/-r/-l/-z/-x/-im/-om/-um/-ns and nasal diphthongs attract final stress. Unmarked -em/-am stay paroxytone (homem, falam).
